task_completion.description: Create a survey titled 'Well-being Study 2025' with 'Group by group' format
task_completion.visual_evidence: LimeSurvey admin interface showing a survey titled 'Well-being Study 2025' and the presentation/format settings set to 'Group by group'.

task_completion.description: Create the SPANEP array question with correct sub-questions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Question editor or survey structure showing question code 'SPANEP' as an Array type, with sub-questions: Positive, Good, Pleasant, Happy, Joyful, Contented, and a 1-5 scale.

task_completion.description: Create the SPANEN array question with correct sub-questions
task_completion.visual_evidence: Question editor or survey structure showing question code 'SPANEN' as an Array type, with sub-questions: Negative, Bad, Unpleasant, Sad, Afraid, Angry, and a 1-5 scale.

task_completion.description: Implement real-time scoring calculations for CalcP, CalcN, and Balance
task_completion.visual_evidence: Survey structure or equation editor showing question codes 'CalcP', 'CalcN', and 'Balance' (typically as Equation question types) with logic to sum the respective arrays and subtract CalcN from CalcP.

task_completion.description: Configure the final display with the required score text
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text display question or survey end message containing exactly: 'Your Positive Score is {CalcP}, Negative Score is {CalcN}, and Affect Balance is {Balance}.'
A green 'Active' badge, a 'Stop this survey' button, or a status indicator showing the survey has been successfully activated.

integrity.description: Agent used the LimeSurvey web GUI to create the survey
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the LimeSurvey admin interface (admin/Admin123!), rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual application elements
integrity.visual_evidence: LimeSurvey menus, question editors, and save buttons are visible and interacted with, not just text typed into a notepad or script file.

integrity.description: Agent used variable placeholders for the final display rather than hardcoded numbers
integrity.visual_evidence: The final display configuration shows curly braces like {CalcP}, {CalcN}, and {Balance} rather than static numbers (e.g., not 'Your Positive Score is 25').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The survey title must contain the keyword 'Well-being'. The required question codes are exactly ['SPANEP', 'SPANEN', 'CalcP', 'CalcN', 'Balance']. The SPANE-P array must contain exactly these sub-questions: Positive, Good, Pleasant, Happy, Joyful, Contented. The SPANE-N array must contain exactly these sub-questions: Negative, Bad, Unpleasant, Sad, Afraid, Angry.
